name: Laniakea (Turtle Beach)
tagline: The most famous turtle viewing spot on the island — green sea turtles haul out right on the sand, daily. Better for watching than snorkeling.
parking: Roadside parking only (~50 spots) — dangerous crossing, use caution
bestTime: Noon–3pm when turtles are most active hauling out onto the sand
talkStory: No touching the turtles — it's a federal offense with hefty fines. Volunteers often rope off resting turtles. Best for viewing turtles on the beach, not snorkeling (better snorkel spots elsewhere). The roadside parking requires crossing Kamehameha Hwy — be very careful with kids. A quick 15-min stop on a North Shore loop, not a full-day destination.
